subject: Databases
title: Bucket Pattern
description: A short lesson to help you apply Bucket Pattern.

hook.text: A sensor sends data every second. One document per reading creates millions of tiny documents. Too many.

buildup.text: The bucket pattern groups readings into time buckets. One document per hour holds 3,600 readings in an array.

discovery.text: Fewer documents mean less overhead: fewer indexes, fewer disk seeks, faster range queries.

twist.text: Pick bucket size by access pattern. Too large loads unneeded data. Too small loses the benefit.

climax.text: MongoDB's time series collections automate this pattern. They bucket data by time automatically.

punchline.text: Bucket pattern: group frequent writes into fewer documents. Less overhead.

quiz.question: What does the bucket pattern optimize for?
options: Reducing the number of documents by grouping frequent writes into time-based buckets
Eliminating the need for indexes entirely
